meta.title: fak concept-disambiguation scorecard - crystal clarity across similar-sounding names
meta._doc: Each row is one DISTINCT confusable fak concept, scored on whether it is crystal-clear: one canonical name, a written definition, an explicit line drawn against its siblings, and that line anchored in a doc that exists. The scorecard DISCOVERS the confusable universe from the tree (per family, a token counts only when it spans >= min_files production files, or is a package/dir name, or a doc heading) so coverage cannot be gamed by editing data. The grounding token must appear in the production corpus; the glossary anchor must exist. Regenerate the doc: python tools/concept_disambiguation_scorecard.py --markdown-dir docs/concept-disambiguation-scorecard

families.name: Cache family
families.note: The headline confusable space: raw KV tensor storage vs the page-table virtualization (vCache) vs the provider prompt-cache vs the metadata sidecar (cachemeta).

families.name: Attention family
families.note: Attention compute vs the attention-witness accumulation (Attended/EMA/Cumulative) vs the DSA AttentionIndex metadata.

families.name: Guard / gate family
families.note: The kernel (guard) vs the many decision points (gate): adjudicators pre-call, result admitters post-call, git-hook gates, promotion gates.

families.name: Session / scheduling family
families.note: Session (the drive record) vs Turn (one round-trip) vs Slot (the free/busy signal) vs Scheduler (the policy that picks the next winner).

families.name: Witness / evidence family
families.note: A world-state witness (a cache's external binding) vs a measurement witness (RSI proof) vs a Claim (self-report) vs the WitnessResolver that corroborates claims.

families.name: Gateway / engine family
families.note: Kernel (coordinator) vs gateway (the wire) vs engine (the dispatch seam) vs vDSO (the local fast path) vs model (the in-kernel forward pass).

families.name: Policy / authorization family
families.note: Capability floor (intent) vs policy manifest (on-disk JSON) vs loaded Policy (compiled table) vs the adjudicator (enforcer) vs verdict/reason-code (its output).

families.name: Context-management family
families.note: context-MMU (gates result bytes) vs KV-MMU (evicts K/V spans) vs recall (the persisted core dump) vs compaction (provider prefix reuse) vs the ctx* planners.

families.name: Scorecard / debt family
families.note: The meta-family: the scorecard machine vs each *_debt integer vs the control pane that folds them - this very scorecard is one member.
